title: Nominalization
summary: German frequently turns verbs and adjectives into nouns (nominalization), especially in formal and written language. Mastering nominalization is essential for academic, professional, and journalistic registers.

sections.heading: Nominalizing Verbs: Infinitive as Noun
body: Any German infinitive can be used as a noun by capitalizing it. Nominalized infinitives are always neuter (das) and take the genitive '-s' as needed.
They typically describe an activity or process in the abstract: das Lesen (reading), das Schreiben (writing), das Denken (thinking), das Lernen (learning), das Reisen (travelling).
As nouns, they can be the subject, object, or complement of a sentence and can take the full range of German case endings on their article.
They may also take a genitive attribute: das Schreiben des Berichts (the writing of the report).

examples.de: Durch das regelmäßige Üben verbessert man seine Fähigkeiten.
examples.en: Through regular practice one improves one's skills.

examples.de: Das Denken in Alternativen ist eine wichtige Kompetenz.
examples.en: Thinking in alternatives is an important competency.

examples.de: Nach dem Schreiben des Berichts fühlte sie sich erleichtert.
examples.en: After writing the report, she felt relieved.

sections.heading: Nominal Style vs. Verbal Style in Formal Writing
body: Formal German—particularly in academic, legal, and bureaucratic writing—strongly favours nominal style (Nominalstil), in which actions are expressed through nouns rather than finite verbs.
This results in longer noun phrases with fewer main verbs, conveying precision and objectivity. However, overuse can reduce readability.
Verbal style (Verbalstil) is more direct and clearer, and is preferred in journalism and modern plain-language writing.
Recognising both styles and being able to convert between them is a key C1 skill.

examples.de: Nominal: Die Durchführung der Analyse ergab überraschende Resultate.
examples.en: The carrying out of the analysis yielded surprising results.

examples.de: Verbal: Als die Analyse durchgeführt wurde, ergaben sich überraschende Resultate.
examples.en: When the analysis was carried out, surprising results emerged.

examples.de: Nominal: Die Inanspruchnahme dieser Dienstleistung setzt eine Registrierung voraus.
examples.en: The use of this service requires registration.

examples.de: Verbal: Um diese Dienstleistung zu nutzen, müssen Sie sich registrieren.
examples.en: To use this service, you must register.

sections.heading: Common Nominalization Suffixes
body: Many German nouns are formed from verbs or adjectives using derivational suffixes. The suffix determines the gender.

-ung (feminine): added to verb stems to denote a process or result — die Lösung (solution, from lösen), die Entwicklung (development, from entwickeln), die Erfahrung (experience, from erfahren), die Verbesserung (improvement, from verbessern).

-heit / -keit (feminine): added to adjectives — die Freiheit (freedom, from frei), die Möglichkeit (possibility, from möglich), die Einheit (unity, from ein), die Schwierigkeit (difficulty, from schwierig).
-nis (neuter or feminine): die Erkenntnis (insight), das Ergebnis (result), die Kenntnis (knowledge), das Hindernis (obstacle).
-schaft (feminine): denotes a collective, state, or relationship — die Gesellschaft (society), die Freundschaft (friendship), die Wissenschaft (science), die Bereitschaft (readiness).

examples.de: Die Vereinfachung der Regeln war notwendig.
examples.en: The simplification of the rules was necessary.

examples.de: Die Bereitschaft zur Zusammenarbeit ist entscheidend.
examples.en: The willingness to cooperate is decisive.

examples.de: Das Ergebnis der Untersuchung liegt nun vor.
examples.en: The result of the investigation is now available.

examples.de: Seine Zuverlässigkeit wird von allen geschätzt.
examples.en: His reliability is appreciated by everyone.

exercises.prompt: Die ___ (entwickeln → -ung noun) neuer Technologien erfordert viel Investition.
exercises.explanation: The verb 'entwickeln' is nominalized with the suffix '-ung' to give 'die Entwicklung' (development). Feminine gender.

exercises.question: Which sentence exemplifies nominal style (Nominalstil)?
options: Weil das Gesetz geändert wurde, mussten alle Verträge angepasst werden.
Die Gesetzesänderung hatte die Anpassung aller Verträge zur Folge.
Man änderte das Gesetz, also musste man alle Verträge anpassen.
Das Gesetz wurde geändert und deshalb wurden die Verträge angepasst.
exercises.explanation: Option B uses two nominalized nouns ('Gesetzesänderung', 'Anpassung') and no subordinate clause — the hallmark of nominal style.

exercises.prompt: Das ___ (lesen) anspruchsvoller Texte schult das kritische Denken.
exercises.explanation: The infinitive 'lesen' is nominalized by capitalizing it → 'das Lesen'. It is always neuter.

exercises.question: What is the correct gender and nominalization suffix for 'zuverlässig' (reliable)?
exercises.explanation: Adjectives ending in '-ig' take the suffix '-keit' to form feminine nouns: die Zuverlässigkeit. Nouns with '-keit' are always feminine.
'Die Durchführung der Analyse der Daten' is a nominal-style noun phrase: 'Durchführung' (nominalization of 'durchführen') + two genitive attributes.
